meta.title: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) — Clean & Inspect Tank T-402
meta.subtitle: Plant 3, Maharashtra — own workers + confined-space-permitted contractors
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. The sign-off block ships with empty signature rows, completed at point of use.

sections.text: Step-by-step Job Safety Analysis for cleaning and inspecting tank T-402 (isolate/lockout -> purge/ventilate -> gas-test -> enter -> clean -> exit & restore) at Plant 3, scored per step on the org 5x5 matrix per ISO 45001 6.1.2. Each step's hazards are energy-source paired, scored, controlled through the hierarchy of controls, and residual-re-scored; the per-step controls are consolidated into SMART actions with named role-label owners and ISO due dates.

items: Job: clean & inspect tank T-402, decomposed into 6 confirmed steps (none invented)
Site: Plant 3, tank farm bay 2, Maharashtra; performed by own workers + confined-space-permitted contractors
Scored PER STEP on the org 5x5 matrix (multiply; Low 1-4 / Medium 5-9 / High 10-15 / Critical 16-25)
Standard: ISO 45001 6.1.2 (hazard identification, per step) + 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls)

sections.text: JSA table (the core — one row per step)

rows: Stored mechanical & process energy on agitator/inlet (mechanical, pressure)
Eliminate: LOTO de-energise + bleed (Elimination); verify zero-energy (Administrative)
Engineering: forced-air ventilation + continuous O2 monitoring; Administrative: purge procedure
Engineering: calibrated multi-gas detector; Administrative: test-before-entry hold point
Confined-space engulfment / atmosphere change on entry (chemical, gravity)
Engineering: continuous monitoring + retrieval system; Administrative: entry permit + standby attendant + rescue plan
Residual hydrocarbon vapour + sludge engulfment (chemical, kinetic)
Engineering: continuous ventilation; Substitution: low-VOC cleaning method; Administrative: limited dwell time
Trip/fall on egress; premature re-energise (gravity, mechanical)
Administrative: controlled egress + LOTO removal sequence + permit close-out
sections.caption: JSA table — one row per confirmed step; the initial/residual cells are coloured off the risk_matrix band. This table IS the JSA.

sections.text: Hierarchy of controls — consolidated control plan

rows.control: LOTO de-energise + bleed before any intervention (addresses Step 1)
Substitute a low-VOC cleaning method for the sludge removal (addresses Step 5)
Continuous forced-air ventilation + continuous gas monitoring (addresses Steps 2, 4, 5)
Entry permit + standby attendant + rescue plan (addresses Step 4)

rows.action: Install a fixed continuous gas monitor on T-402 with entry interlock (Steps 3,4)
Qualify and adopt a low-VOC sludge-cleaning method (Step 5)
Revise the confined-space entry SOP + rescue plan for T-402 (Step 4)

items: ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 (hazard identification & assessment of risks, applied per step) + 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls)
Maharashtra — the resolved state accident/dangerous-occurrence form via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS (state confirmed; never a national form number)
Permit-to-work triggers implied by the steps: confined-space entry permit (Step 4), hot-work permit if any (none in this job)

sections.text: This JSA must be reviewed and accepted before the job starts. Role-labelled rows are signed at point of use; names are entered on the physical/printed copy, never carried into this de-identified document.

sections.caption: Sign-off / acceptance block (CORE-02 / SC-1) — role-labelled rows with EMPTY signature + date columns, completed at point of use; no fabricated names (de-id convention).
